menu.name: Batch Media Optimizer
menu.label: Open Optimizer
menu.hint: Scan the world and convert images to WebP (and video to WebM), then repoint references.

quality.name: WebP quality
quality.hint: Lossy WebP quality from 0.3 to 1.0. Higher keeps more detail at a larger size. Used by both backfill and upload-time conversion.

maxDimension.name: Max dimension (px)
maxDimension.hint: Downscale images and video whose longest edge exceeds this many pixels. 0 disables downscaling. WebP's hard limit is 16383px.

losslessPngAlphaUnder.name: Lossless PNG-alpha under (px)
losslessPngAlphaUnder.hint: PNGs with transparency whose longest edge is under this size are encoded losslessly (good for tokens/icons). 0 disables.

videoQuality.name: Video quality
videoQuality.hint: VP9 quality from 0.3 to 1.0, mapped to a bits-per-pixel bitrate target. Higher keeps more detail at a larger size. Used for MP4/MOV → WebM conversion during backfill.

audioQuality.name: Audio quality
audioQuality.hint: Opus quality from 0.3 to 1.0, mapped to a bitrate target. Higher keeps more detail at a larger size. Used for audio → Ogg/Opus conversion during backfill.

uploadOptimize.name: Optimize on upload
uploadOptimize.hint: Automatically convert PNG/JPG images to WebP as they are uploaded through Foundry.

includeItems.name: Include item art
includeItems.hint: Also optimize world item images and actor-owned item images during backfill.

includeImages.name: Include images
includeImages.hint: Convert PNG/JPG images to WebP during backfill. Untick (with video on) for a video-only pass.

includeVideo.name: Include video
includeVideo.hint: Also convert MP4/MOV video (scene backgrounds, video tiles, animated tokens) to WebM during backfill. Encoding is much slower than images; audio is dropped.

includeAudio.name: Include audio
includeAudio.hint: Also convert audio (playlist sounds, scene ambient sounds) to Ogg/Opus during backfill. Note: Ogg plays in Foundry's client and Chrome/Firefox, but not Safari.

includePackages.name: Convert module/system assets (risky)
includePackages.hint: Off by default. When on, linked media under modules/ and systems/ is also converted during backfill. A package update can overwrite the original and remove the converted twin, breaking the reference. Only document-linked media is touched; core Foundry assets are never converted.

app.title: Batch Media Optimizer
app.mediaHint: Untick types to run a subset — e.g. images off + audio on for an audio-only pass.
app.scopePackages: Also convert module/system assets (at your own risk)
app.packagesHint: Off by default. When on, linked media shipped under modules/ and systems/ is also converted. Risk: a module/system update can overwrite the original and remove the converted twin, breaking the reference. Only linked media is touched; core Foundry assets are never converted.
app.videoNote: Video re-encoding (VP9) is hardware-accelerated but still far slower than images — expect seconds to minutes per clip. Audio is dropped.
app.audioNote: Audio is re-encoded to Opus and wrapped in Ogg. Best efficiency and broad support; not playable in Safari.
app.repointFailHint: The optimized file was written, but these document fields could not be repointed (the write was rejected or silently ignored). Their references still point at the original. Re-link manually, or report the field name shown.
app.cleanupHint: These originals now have an optimized twin and are no longer referenced. Foundry has no file-delete API, so remove them manually once you have verified the world.
app.confirmBody: This will upload optimized copies (WebP/WebM) and update document references. Take a fresh world backup first. Originals are kept. Proceed?
app.cancelled: Run cancelled. Already-written files were kept.
